# MD Feedback

> VS Code extension and MCP server for reviewing markdown plans before AI agents implement them. Annotate with Fix, Question, and Highlight. AI agents read annotations directly through Model Context Protocol (MCP).

## Overview

MD Feedback bridges the gap between plan creation and AI implementation. Instead of reviewing code after it is written, MD Feedback enables plan review — catching architecture mistakes and missing requirements at the design stage, before any code is produced.

The tool consists of two components:
1. VS Code Extension (yeominux.md-feedback-vscode) — interactive annotation editor
2. MCP Server (npm: md-feedback) — lets AI agents read annotations programmatically

## Key Facts

- 3 annotation types: Highlight (reading mark), Fix (needs change), Question (needs clarification)
- 13 MCP tools for agent integration
- Export to 11 AI tools: Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex, Cline, Windsurf, Roo Code, Gemini, Antigravity, Generic, Handoff
- Annotations stored as portable HTML comments in the markdown file — no proprietary format, no cloud dependency
- Quality gates with automatic evaluation (blocked/proceed/done)
- Session handoffs preserve context across AI agent sessions
- Checkpoints track review progress over time
- Plan cursor tracks current position in a document
- Keyboard shortcuts: press 1 (highlight), 2 (fix), 3 (question)
- Rich rendering: Mermaid diagrams, callout blocks, syntax-highlighted code
- Free for personal and non-commercial use (SUL-1.0 license)

## How It Works

1. Write a plan in markdown
2. Open in MD Feedback sidebar in VS Code — annotate with highlights, fixes, questions
3. AI agent reads annotations via MCP — no export step needed
4. Agent implements fixes, answers questions, marks memos done
5. Gates auto-evaluate — agent knows when all items are resolved
6. Agent generates handoff — next session picks up where you left off

## Design Philosophy

- Humans only state what is wrong. AI decides how to fix it.
- Three annotation types are sufficient — AI infers intent from context.
- Markdown is the source of truth. All state lives in the file itself.
- Annotations are HTML comments — portable, git-friendly, editor-agnostic.
- Zero cognitive load: passive status display, no extra decisions required.

## MCP Server Tools

- get_document_structure: full review state (memos, gates, cursor, sections, summary)
- list_annotations: all annotations with type, status, owner, color
- get_review_status: annotation counts and session status
- create_annotation: create annotation programmatically with anchor search
- respond_to_memo: add AI response to an annotation
- update_memo_status: mark memo as open, answered, or wontfix
- update_cursor: set plan cursor position
- evaluate_gates: check if quality conditions are met
- export_review: export for a specific AI tool format
- create_checkpoint: save review progress snapshot
- get_checkpoints: list all checkpoints
- generate_handoff: create structured handoff document
- pickup_handoff: parse existing handoff for session resumption

## Use Cases

- Reviewing AI-generated implementation plans before coding begins
- Structured feedback for AI coding agents (replaces unstructured chat messages)
- Session continuity across multiple AI agent sessions via handoffs
- Quality gate enforcement for AI-driven workflows
- Team plan review with portable annotations (HTML comments survive git)
- Vibe coding workflow: plan first, review, then let AI build

## Installation

VS Code: Install "MD Feedback" from VS Code Marketplace (publisher: yeominux)
MCP: npx md-feedback (no install needed)

MCP config for any compatible client:
{ "mcpServers": { "md-feedback": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "md-feedback"] } } }

## FAQ

What is MD Feedback?
A VS Code extension and MCP server for reviewing markdown plans before AI agents implement them. Annotate text, press 1/2/3, and AI agents read your structured feedback directly via MCP.

What is plan review?
Plan review means reviewing designs and plans before implementation. Unlike code review (after code is written), plan review catches mistakes at the design stage.

What is MCP?
Model Context Protocol (MCP) lets AI agents interact with external tools. MD Feedback's MCP server gives agents direct access to annotations without manual export.

Which AI tools are supported?
Claude Code (CLAUDE.md), Cursor (.cursor/rules/), GitHub Copilot (.github/copilot-instructions.md), Codex (AGENTS.md), Cline (.clinerules), Windsurf (.windsurfrules), Roo Code (.roo/rules/), Gemini (.gemini/styleguide.md), Antigravity (.agent/rules/), Generic markdown, and Handoff documents.

Does it work offline?
Yes. All annotations are stored locally in the markdown file. The MCP server runs locally via npx.

Is it free?
Free for personal and non-commercial use under the SUL-1.0 license.

## Links

- VS Code Marketplace: https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=yeominux.md-feedback-vscode
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/md-feedback
